seoTitle: Phegans Bay vs Phillip Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Phegans Bay vs Phillip Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Phegans Bay and Phillip Bay.
heading: Phegans Bay vs Phillip Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Phegans Bay, New South Wales and Phillip Bay, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Phillip Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Phegans Bay (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Phegans Bay or Phillip Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Phegans Bay has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Phillip Bay scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Phegans Bay scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Phillip Bay scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Phegans Bay or Phillip Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Phillip Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
